title: An Attempt to Understand the teaching of Not-Self.To play the media you will need to either update your browser to a recent version or update yourFlash plugin
justification: This talk offers the most comprehensive and systematic exploration of not-self and identity, combining philosophical exposition with practical meditation guidance. It covers the historical context of 'atta,' the five aggregates analysis, dependent origination, the developmental psychology of self-formation from infancy, and concrete meditative exercises for investigating identity. The talk has excellent literary potential with its clear structure, accessible language, and the teacher's honest acknowledgment of the teaching's difficulty, making it an ideal spine for a chapter on this challenging topic.
summary: Bhante Bodhidhamma systematically unpacks the Buddha's teaching on not-self by examining what 'atta' (soul/self) meant historically, analyzing the five aggregates for permanence and control, and tracing how identity forms from infancy. He concludes with practical meditation exercises for directly investigating the observer and experiencing the teaching beyond intellectual understanding.

relevance.note: Directly examines anattā teaching and soul concepts
